title: Competitive Game Theory (Chess, Go, Poker)

chess_game_tree.reason: Chess is finite — perfect information, decidable, EML-0 in principle

chess_evaluation.reason: Log-linear evaluation scores; ELO is logarithmic

chess_opening_theory.reason: Opening battles = strategic oscillation between attack/defense modes

go_complexity.description: Go: 10^{170} positions, no known analytic solution
go_complexity.reason: Computational irreducibility — no finite EML description of optimal play

poker_expectation.reason: Expected value = linear in probabilities; bluffing is log-odds

poker_bluffing.reason: Mixed Nash equilibrium: exp(utility) softmax — oscillation between strategies

elo_rating.reason: Logistic function of log-rating difference — EML-2

analysis.computational_tractability: Does the framework predict which games are computationally tractable? YES. EML depth predicts tractability: EML-0: decidable in principle (Chess, Checkers — solved). EML-2: tractable with logarithmic heuristics (evaluations, ELO). EML-∞: computationally irreducible — no polynomial shortcut (Go, general game). Go = EML-∞ because its optimal evaluation function has no finite EML description. AlphaGo works by approximating EML-∞ with a large EML-2/3 neural network.

analysis.verdict: Chess: EML-0 (solvable). Go: EML-∞ (irreducible). ELO/poker: EML-2.
analysis.theorem: T232: Game Tractability — EML depth predicts computational tractability

key_theorem: T232: Game Tractability (S511). Chess: EML-0 (finite, decidable). ELO/evaluation: EML-2 (log). Poker mixed strategy: EML-3. Go: EML-∞ (computationally irreducible). EML depth predicts tractability: EML-finite → tractable with heuristics; EML-∞ → irreducible.
rabbit_hole_log: Chess: finite game tree → EML-0 (decidable in principle)
Poker Nash equilibrium: softmax mixed strategy → EML-3
Go: no finite evaluation function → EML-∞ irreducibility
T232: EML depth = tractability classifier for games
